title: Addressing epistasis in the design of protein function
key_findings: Epistatic interactions significantly impact protein engineering outcomes
Nonadditive effects of mutations create design bottlenecks
Machine learning improves prediction of epistatic effects
Context-dependent epistasis limits generalization across proteins

epistasis_quantification.magnitude: 30-60% of double mutation effects are epistatic
epistasis_quantification.patterns: Both positive and negative epistasis observed
epistasis_quantification.prediction_accuracy: Machine learning improves over additive models

practical_implications.design_strategy: Account for epistasis in library design
practical_implications.screening_prioritization: Focus on mutation combinations, not individual mutations
practical_implications.model_accuracy: Improves from 0.6 to 0.8+ R² with epistasis terms
